records.ety: [L. insensibilis: cf. F. insensible. See In- not, and Sensible.]

defs: Destitute of the power of feeling or perceiving; wanting bodily sensibility; unconscious.
Not susceptible of emotion or passion; void of feeling; apathetic; unconcerned; indifferent; as, insensible to danger, fear, love, etc.; -- often used with of or to.
quotes: Accept an obligation without being a slave to the giver, or insensible to his kindness.
Lost in their loves, insensible of shame.

defs: Incapable of being perceived by the senses; imperceptible. Hence: Progressing by imperceptible degrees; slow; gradual; as, insensible motion.
quotes: Two small and almost insensible pricks were found upon Cleopatra's arm.
They fall away, And languish with insensible decay.
If it make the indictment be insensible or uncertain, it shall be quashed.

defs: Incapable of feeling a specific sensation or emotion; as, insensible to pity.
